title: Ipomoea cordillerae
taxonomy: Ipomoea cordillerae is classified within the family Convolvulaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the morning glory family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its growth habit, leaf structure, and flower morphology. The genus Ipomoea is further divided into several species, with Ipomoea cordillerae being a distinct species within this group. The classification of Ipomoea cordillerae is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies, which provide additional evidence of its relationship to other species within the Convolvulaceae family. As a result, the classification of Ipomoea cordillerae as a member of the Convolvulaceae family is well-established in botanical literature.
uses: Ipomoea cordillerae has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Its roots are also thought to possess antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in treating infections. intro: Ipomoea cordillerae, also known as Cordilleran morning glory, is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea cordillerae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
